disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No mucocutaneous bleeding — no petechiae/purpura/new bruising, no epistaxis, gum, urinary or GI bleeding ('dry' picture)
trigger_question_examples: Any pinprick red spots, new bruises, nosebleeds, bleeding gums, or blood in your urine/stool?
When you check your skin, do you see any tiny red spots?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No red spots or new bruises, no nosebleeds or bleeding gums, and no blood in my urine or stool.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Headache is band-like/tension-type (not thunderclap); no focal weakness, speech change, or vomiting (no intracranial haemorrhage)
trigger_question_examples: Is the headache a sudden 'worst ever' explosive one, or a band-like tension ache?
Any weakness, drooping, slurred speech, or vomiting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a band-like tension ache, not sudden or explosive, and no weakness, drooping, slurred speech or vomiting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began on reading the platelet result; count ~75 (safe); hyperventilating with prior health anxiety
trigger_question_examples: When did the headache start relative to reading the result?
Does this feel like previous health-anxiety episodes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started when I read the result; the count was about 75 and it feels like my past health anxiety.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Am I bleeding into my brain? Do I need an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain accurately that a platelet count of ~75 carries a very low spontaneous-bleeding risk (serious bleeding is rare above ~10-20), screen mucocutaneous bleeding and focal neurology/thunderclap headache, and if reassuringly negative, de-escalate the panic and treat the tension headache — with a clear bleeding/neurological safety-net, without either dismissing or over-escalating.
